methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.4B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Neura Robotics is a Series C-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $1.4B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public entity and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage band, including Hadrian, Nimble Robotics, Exotec, Dexterity, and Bedrock Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $1.4B total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $2.4B. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Nimble Robotics) to $2.0B (Exotec). The peer set reflects diverse stage positioning: one Series D, two Series B, and two Series C entities.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $1.4B (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median estimate of $2.0B. The spread reflects genuine uncertainty around Neura's competitive positioning within the peer cohort and stage-typical growth expectations. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 1.7x step-up multiplier to this company's specific risk profile and whether peer comps fully capture sector-specific capital intensity or technology differentiation.
